title: malus domestica var. dasyphylla
intro: Malus domestica var. dasyphylla, also known as the crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a variant of the domestic apple (Malus domestica). The crabapple has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of the crabapple include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other herbal preparations to harness its medicinal and nutritional properties.
